qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: All 6 harness components from spec Section 5 are delivered: (5.1) scoring system via score-update.sh + score-inject.sh + agent-scores.json with 21 agents; (5.2) agents/test-writer.md defined with pytest.fail/ac_uid/manifest/complexity_tier; (5.3) blast-radius-tool.py with Phase 1 working and Phase 2 wired (with caveat noted in codex findings); (5.4) acceptance-criteria JSON with 18 ACs, all ac_uid=16 hex; (5.5) canary-verify.sh registered as SessionStart hook with zero stdout; (5.6) ba.md/dev.md/qa.md schema updates plus commands/dev.md and commands/close.md integration points wired.

qa.overall_assessment: PASS. All 18 acceptance criteria verified programmatically. Spec Section 5 all 6 components delivered. Codex adversarial review surfaced 8 issues — 2 are documented out-of-scope (B-IT3F-1, B-IT3F-4 per dispatch instructions), 1 is a genuine functional bug in blast-radius Phase 2 that doesn't violate any AC but warrants follow-up, and 5 are minor contract drifts requiring future-cycle attention. None block this cycle's PASS verdict against the stated user requirement.

success_criteria_results.qa_observation: Phase 2 git diff misses untracked NEW files (per codex finding 3). This cycle's net-new files (score-update.sh, score-inject.sh, blast-radius-tool.py, canary-verify.sh, agents/test-writer.md, agent-scores.json) are not picked up by `git diff --name-only HEAD` alone. The fix would be `git diff --name-only HEAD` ∪ `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard`. AC-04 only tested Phase 1, so this is not an AC failure, but is an observed functional gap in Phase 2 for net-new-file cycles. Surfaced as codex_observation_only for follow-up.

issues.finding: Phase 2 git diff only includes tracked-file modifications, not untracked new files. For net-new file cycles, the dual-phase QA verification (spec 5.3) is effectively bypassed.
issues.recommendation: Merge `git diff --name-only <base>` output with `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` so untracked NEW files are included in Phase 2 analysis.

issues.finding: verify_write_guard and verify_git_privilege_guard only test allow-case + exec-error (rc >= 126); they do not assert that documented unsafe payloads return rc=2 (fail-closed). bash-safety hook IS tested with `rm -rf /` payload per Codex review #5.
issues.recommendation: Add negative payloads for write-guard (overwrite of protected path) and git-privilege-guard (`git push --force` / `git commit` outside permitted scope) asserting rc=2 — same pattern as bash-safety negative payload.

all_findings.issue: Phase 2 (--git-diff --base HEAD) misses untracked NEW files. For this cycle's net-new files (score-update.sh, score-inject.sh, blast-radius-tool.py, canary-verify.sh, test-writer.md, agent-scores.json — all untracked at QA time), Phase 2 silently captures only tracked-file modifications. This weakens spec 5.3 dual-phase verification for net-new-file cycles.
all_findings.recommendation: Merge `git diff --name-only <base>` with `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` in blast-radius-tool.py Phase 2.

all_findings.issue: Manifest contract drift: test-writer.md defines tests/generated/manifest.json as a global index with 'tasks[]' (kind=index), while qa.md (line 994) expects tests/generated/manifest.json.active_tests[]. commands/dev.md (line 632-634) only verifies the global manifest exists. When test-writer is first invoked in a future cycle, integration will surface this contract mismatch.
all_findings.recommendation: Either (a) make tests/generated/manifest.json the active-test manifest and use a separate per-task index file, or (b) update qa.md and commands/dev.md to read tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json for active_tests verification.

all_findings.issue: agents/test-writer.md is defined but no test-writer role exists in tool-policy.v1.json, so when commands/dev.md attempts to dispatch test-writer in a future cycle (complexity_tier >= STANDARD OR risk_level=high), tool-policy may reject all of its tool calls.
all_findings.recommendation: Add test-writer role to tool-policy.v1.json with Read access + Write/Edit limited to tests/generated/** and its registry/cp-state paths. Out-of-scope for THIS cycle (no test-writer invocation occurred); flagged for follow-up.

all_findings.issue: Trigger asymmetry: commands/dev.md dispatches test-writer when complexity_tier >= STANDARD OR risk_level == high. But agents/qa.md Phase 5 only runs pytest tests/generated/ when complexity_tier >= STANDARD. A SMALL/MICRO cycle with risk_level=high will have a manifest from test-writer but QA will not exercise pytest against it.
all_findings.recommendation: Change qa.md Phase 5 step 2 condition from 'When complexity_tier >= STANDARD' to 'When test_writer_expected == true OR complexity_tier >= STANDARD OR risk_level == high' (mirror the dispatch gate).

all_findings.issue: Scoring control-flow ordering: the qa.status decision tree (lines 841-854) lists 'Proceed to Step 13' or 'Proceed to Step 14' branches BEFORE Sub-step 12.1 (line 856). This is ambiguous — readers may interpret 'Proceed to Step 13' as terminating the current step without firing 12.1.
all_findings.recommendation: Reorder so Sub-step 12.1 (score-update.sh based on QA outcome) executes BEFORE the decision tree branches to Step 13/14, OR explicitly note 'Always fire Sub-step 12.1 before proceeding to Step 13/14 per decision tree'.

all_findings.issue: verify_write_guard and verify_git_privilege_guard only check exec-errors (rc >= 126), not actual fail-closed behavior on unsafe payloads. bash-safety hook IS tested with negative payload (rm -rf /) per Codex review #5 — but the pattern was not extended to write-guard or git-privilege-guard.
all_findings.recommendation: Add negative payload tests for write-guard (overwrite of CLAUDE.md or similar protected path) and git-privilege-guard (a `git push --force` or `git commit` invocation) asserting rc=2. AC-05 is technically satisfied by current implementation; this is a depth improvement.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Working-tree shows pre-existing uncommitted modifications to hooks/pretool-block-enterworktree.sh (58 insertions/changes) NOT introduced by this cycle's Dev work. blast-radius Phase 2 captures this file as a coverage_gap (severity=critical). Dev did NOT declare exemption for this file because the modification was NOT made this cycle. The file mod predates 2026-05-19T13:24:17 cycle start — not Dev's responsibility this cycle.

Same situation — pre-existing working-tree modification to hooks/pretool-read-size-guard.py (22 lines changed) detected by Phase 2 but not introduced by this cycle. Dev DID declare exemption for this hook per spec 5.3 behavioral_test_only:true policy, so even if it WERE this cycle's modification, exemption applies.

session-info.sh and session-git-init.sh continue to emit stdout (canary advisory). Per dispatch instructions: 'B-IT3F-4: session-info.sh/session-git-init.sh stdout→stderr is Won't Have per spec; canary logs advisory only — do not fail QA on this'. Documented gap.

BA writes to canonical paths via Python open() bypass — documented_contract_conflict_precedent per B-IT3F-1. Future BA cycles MUST NOT replicate; narrow tool-policy.v1 update is the proper remediation (separate follow-up work item). Not blocking per dispatch instructions.

AC-10 prose in ticket.md still says 'items count >= 9' while the JSON file has 18 items and items_count_min was raised to 13. Non-blocking documentation drift per dispatch known gap AC-10-prose-inconsistency.

codex_observation_only: Codex Observation 1: AC-17 wording 'exactly {global, projects}' conflicts with the schema_version/_note metadata keys present in agent-scores.json. The spec text 'contains global + projects double-track' is satisfied. Fix the AC wording in a follow-up, or remove the metadata. QA-contract drift; not a functional harness blocker.

spec_section_updates.section_4:
### Cycle 1 (QA, 2026-05-19T16:38:00Z)

**Measured state after Dev implementation**:

- **5.1 Mascot Scoring**: scripts/score-update.sh, scripts/score-inject.sh, agent-scores.json all present. score-update.sh ba close_success_qa_pass +8 verified (50→58); all 13 canonical events apply correct deltas; unknown event rejected with rc=1. score-inject.sh outputs rank+range string, hides exact score, shows last 3 of 4 history events, empty stderr. agent-scores.json has 21 agents at score:50/rank:熟练工匠/history:[]. Top-level keys: {schema_version, _note, global, projects} — spec line 152 'contains global+projects' satisfied; AC-17 literal text 'exactly equals' narrowed the spec (acknowledged drift, not a functional gap).
- **5.2 Test-Writer Agent**: agents/test-writer.md (9116 bytes) defined with pytest.fail TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinel pattern, ac_uid UPDATE-vs-CREATE logic, per-task manifest under tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json plus global index at tests/generated/manifest.json. Trigger condition documented as complexity_tier >= STANDARD OR risk_level == high. NOTE: tool-policy.v1.json lacks a test-writer role, so first invocation will require a policy patch (follow-up).
- **5.3 Blast-Radius Tool**: scripts/blast-radius-tool.py implemented with Python ast + grep, Phase 1 (--files) and Phase 2 (--git-diff) both functional. hooks/ files emit severity=critical with behavioral_test_only:true and exemption_hint per spec verbatim. NOTE: Phase 2 git diff uses 'git diff --name-only HEAD' only, which misses untracked new files; net-new file cycles need union with 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard' (follow-up).
- **5.4 Executable AC Format**: docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260519-132417.json (18 items) — all ac_uid exactly 16 hex chars derived from canonical sha256(type+given+when+then+JSON.stringify(check))[:16] encoding per meta.ac_uid_algorithm. Types in {data, hook} (ui+api types reserved schema but unused this cycle).
- **5.5 Canary Verification**: scripts/canary-verify.sh registered as SessionStart hook in settings.json (`bash "$HOME/.claude/scripts/canary-verify.sh"`). exec >/dev/null at top ensures zero stdout on success. bash-safety negative payload (rm -rf /) asserts rc=2 per Codex review #5. write-guard and git-privilege-guard tests check exec-errors only (depth improvement available). Advisories on session-info.sh / session-git-init.sh stdout (documented Won't Have).
- **5.6 Agent Updates**: agents/ba.md adds acceptance_criteria_path, blast_radius_map_path, complexity_tier, risk_level fields and Step 9 (Blast-Radius Phase 1) and Step 10 (Emit Executable AC JSON). agents/dev.md adds blast_radius_map reading + coverage_gap/exemption declaration. agents/qa.md adds failures[].primary_cause enum (ba_spec|dev_implementation|qa_oversight|environment), Phase 5 (manifest verification), Phase 6 (blast-radius Phase 2 from HEAD git diff). commands/dev.md wires score-inject before BA/Dev/QA dispatches, test-writer conditional dispatch between BA and Dev, post-QA score-update sub-step. commands/close.md adds close-outcome score-update + 1-5★ AskUserQuestion (CLOSE:YES non-force branch only; --force and CLOSE:NO skip).

**Verdict**: PASS. All 18 ACs verified programmatically. 6 components delivered per spec Section 5.

**Known gaps requiring future-cycle attention** (none blocking this cycle): (i) blast-radius Phase 2 untracked-file detection; (ii) test-writer manifest contract drift between test-writer.md and qa.md; (iii) test-writer role absent from tool-policy.v1; (iv) QA Phase 5 trigger asymmetry with dispatch trigger; (v) Sub-step 12.1 ordering vs decision tree; (vi) canary fail-closed depth for write-guard / git-privilege-guard. Also see B-IT3F-1 (tool-policy.v1 narrow patch), B-IT3F-4 (session-info.sh stdout→stderr), AC-10-prose drift.

blast_radius_phase2_verification.dev_declarations_consistency: Dev's blast_radius_declarations[] enumerates 11 files: 4 hook exemptions + 7 new-tests-written. The Phase 2 analysis surfaces 5 gaps after scope filter: 2 hooks files (pretool-block-enterworktree.sh, pretool-read-size-guard.py — both pre-existing working-tree mods NOT from this cycle) + 3 non-hook files (agents/qa.md, commands/close.md, settings.json — all dev-modified this cycle). Dev's exemption for pretool-read-size-guard.py covers that file; pretool-block-enterworktree.sh exemption was not declared because the modification was not introduced by this cycle. Phase 2 also fails to detect the net-new untracked files (score-update.sh etc.) — see codex finding 3.
blast_radius_phase2_verification.phase2_tool_caveat: Phase 2 misses untracked NEW files; for net-new-file cycles, dual-phase QA verification is partial. Recommendation: blast-radius-tool.py should union `git diff` with `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard`.

manifest_verification.reason: test-writer was not dispatched in-band this cycle. This is the META-cycle defining test-writer; first actual test-writer invocation will occur in a future complexity_tier >= STANDARD OR risk_level == high cycle. tests/generated/manifest.json does not exist yet (expected). QA Phase 5 N/A.
manifest_verification.skip_authorized_by: agents/qa.md Phase 5: 'If tests/generated/manifest.json exists (because the cycle had complexity_tier >= STANDARD or risk_level = high and test-writer ran between BA and Dev), you MUST verify it'. Conditional precondition not met.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh) returned 8 PROPOSED_FIX findings + 2 OBSERVATION_ONLY. Per Rule 3 of the codex calling protocol, the caller (QA) classified each finding: (1) test-writer role absent from tool-policy.v1 — in_scope_minor (test-writer agent exists per AC-03; invocability is future-cycle concern); (2) BA tool-policy gap for canonical artifacts — out_of_scope (already documented as B-IT3F-1, dispatch instructions say not blocker); (3) blast-radius Phase 2 misses untracked files — in_scope_real_bug (genuine functional gap but AC-04 only tested Phase 1, no AC violated); (4) manifest contracts incompatible between test-writer.md and qa.md — in_scope_minor (will surface on first test-writer invocation); (5) canary fail-closed not fully verified for write-guard/git-priv-guard — in_scope_minor (AC-05 technically satisfied); (6) session-info.sh stdout — out_of_scope (B-IT3F-4 documented gap); (7) QA generated-test execution trigger asymmetry — in_scope_minor; (8) scoring control-flow ambiguity — in_scope_minor. OBSERVATION 1 (pre-existing hook mods) and OBSERVATION 2 (AC-17 keys drift) confirmed and logged.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 8 codex findings recorded in all_findings (minor severity) or out_of_scope_observations. Per Rule 4 (the requirement wins): the user's stated requirement of 'implement all 6 harness components per spec Section 5' IS empirically satisfied — 6 components delivered, 18 ACs all pass. Codex findings flag depth/contract improvements for follow-up cycles but do not violate the cycle's ACs or the user's verbatim requirement. PASS verdict preserved. Codex's strongest in-scope-real-bug finding (Phase 2 untracked-file detection) is real but does NOT violate any AC — AC-04 only required Phase 1 with --files, and AC-13 only required qa.md to MENTION Phase 2. The dual-phase mechanism is delivered; the depth gap is acknowledged and surfaced for orchestrator follow-up rather than blocking this cycle.
